response.justification: Szabo brings valuable geospatial domain expertise and excellent engineering practices (TDD, testing frameworks), but has a significant gap in the primary requirement: his modern C++ experience is 6+ years outdated, with his recent 5 years focused on JavaScript/TypeScript full-stack development. He demonstrates strong learning ability and comfort across tech stacks, but would require substantial onboarding on systems-level C++ work for a mid-senior IC role. Interview worthwhile if the company can invest in C++ ramp-up and values his GIS background; skip if productivity in modern C++ is critical from day one.
strengths: Deep geospatial and GIS domain expertise through 10+ years of work including open-source GISCollective platform and FOSS4G contributions
Exceptional software engineering discipline: extensive TDD practice, created testing frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and led testing workshops across multiple companies
Full-stack versatility and demonstrated comfort learning new languages and domains across web, systems, and embedded contexts
concerns: Modern C++ skills are severely outdated: last significant C++ work was 2014–2018 in different domains; recent 5+ years dominated by JavaScript/TypeScript web development
No Rust experience despite it being an acceptable alternative for the primary role requirement
GIS/mapping domain experience is valuable but distinct from routing systems; navigation systems work is limited to mapping context, not real-time traffic or large-scale routing optimization

key_factors.factor: Proven testing discipline and engineering rigor
